Off Calendar Feb-26-2013 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT
Off Calendar Dec-03-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Rothstein for Bank of America. Stephen Picone appeared by telephone for Donald Amos.

Counsel had nothing to add. Mr. Rothstein explained that the Third Account will be on the Survivor's Trust and the Redisual Trust for the first half of 2010. He will then file the second half of 2010, 2011 and 2012 for the seperate Trusts for Donald and for Douglas. The Court granted both of the accounting petitions (subject to review of the orders). Status on filing of the accounting on the first half of 2010 to the date of seperation of the trust was set for February 26, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Rothstein stated that a Special Needs Trust was established for Douglas in 2010.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy C. Garcia.

Off Calendar Oct-02-2012 Continued To Nov-06-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stephen Picone appeared by CourtCall for Donald Amos. Gary Rothstein for Bank of America (Trustee). Ernest Der (Guardian Ad Litem for Douglas Amos).

Counsel addressed the issues raised by the Examiner's Notes with regard to allocation of fees between principal and income, the trusts for Donald and Douglas, Mr. Picone's fees and how they are allocated and $1.9 millions held in one money market account. The Court needs a schedule of the allocation. The matter was continued to November 6, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further filings.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

Petition For Approval Of Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Sep-21-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for Douglas Amos. Amanda Casillas for Donald Amos.

Ms. Casillas explained the extent of services provided to support the fee request. Mr. Der stated that he was here to make sure that Donald's fees were allocated to his share of the Trust. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

Petition For Approval Of Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Aug-23-2010 Continued To Sep-13-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For An Order Directing Payment Of Statutory Lien Claim
Off Calendar Aug-23-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Beland for Departmant of Developemental Services (Claimant). Steve Picone for Petitioner. Eugene Hahm for Bank of America (Trustee). Ernest Der Guardian Ad Litem for Douglas Amos.

Mr. Beland and Mr. Hahm presented oral argument to support their postions with regard to the Petition for Order Directing Payment of Statutory Lien Claim. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court took the matter off calendar stating that it was mooted by the Court's ruling on the Petition for Instructions filed by the Trustee, and the rulings on the Petition to Set Aside Order and the Petition for Instructions filed by the Department of Developemental Services. Mr. Picone presented oral argument to support the fee request. The Court stated it had concerns about the amount of fees, that the fees were high, that there was no breakdown of the services and the hourly fees, and requested Mr. Picone file a further declaration stating what work was performed by him at his hourly rate and the work that was performed by his Associate at the lower hourly rate. The Petition for Approval of Attorney's Fees was continued to September 13, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Approval Of Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Aug-17-2010 Continued To Aug-23-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Order; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Bruce J. Beland
Off Calendar Aug-12-2010 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Beland for Department of Developmental Services.

The Court informed Mr. Beland that the Probate Examiner received a phone call from opposing Counsel stating that he did not receive a phone call from Mr. Beland indicating that he was coming in to argue the tentative ruling. Mr. Beland stated that he did not notify opposing Counsel of his intent to argue. The Court cited/referred to local rule 8.3 D and adopted the tentative ruling. The Motion To Set Aside Order is denied.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

Motion To Set Aside Order
Off Calendar May-27-2010 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der Guardian Ad Litem for Douglas Amos. Eugene Hahm and Gary Rothstein for Bank of America (Trustee). Amanda Casilla for Donald Amos. Bruce Beland for Department of Developmental Services.

The Court explained that the motion filed on March 15, 2010 does not comply with CCP 1010 or with CRC 3.111 and stated that the motion must indicate the grounds for relief. The Court adopted the tentative as follows: The Motion is Denied without prejudice (order for the same signed). Mr. Beland stated that he intended to re-file the motion within 10 days. A hearing date of August 12, 2010 has been reserved for said motion. The Petition for an Order Directing Payment of Statutory Lien Claim on calendar for June 7, 2010 will be continued to a later date after the motion is heard and the Court will determine if the accounting petition needs to be continued as well.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

Petition For Qualified Severance Of Irrevocable Trust.
Off Calendar Mar-03-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stephen Picone for Donald Amos. Ernest Der for Douglas Amos (Guardian Ad Litem). Gary Rothstein for Bank of America.

There being no oppostion to the Petition For Qualified Severance Of Irrevocable Trust or to the Petition For Instructions Re Payment of Special Needs Trust Funds to The Department of Developemental Services, the Court will grant both said petitions. Mr. Rothstein stated that the Trustee believed that the Trust would get a better return, that the house was currently vacant, that it was the Broker's opinion that property value was on the rise and that the matter had been going on for one year. The Petition for Instructions Regarding Sale of Residence was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy D. Sum.
